channel.title: Comments for page "Midnight Parade"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Midnight Parade"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this, +1.

Now I'm off to read your other Alagadda tales. :)

The constant frown on his face relived slightly

I think this is meant to say "relieved".

There was no scream, but even if there was, it could not be heard.

I suggest: "There were no screams, but even if there had been, they would not have been heard."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. I like the action and the implications, and I really enjoy people playing around in the style of Poe and Chambers, and, to some extent, Hawthorne (seriously folks, go read that story, it's awesome.)

As far as that style goes, it's not perfect. There's occasionally some odd grammatical structure that seems faux-archaic rather than genuine, and some visual flourishes that seemed to be there to meet a purple prose quota, rather than being really layered into the bones of the piece.

Not meaning to beat up the piece too much. I did really enjoy it, and writing in the voices of others comes easier with practice. +1

(Also, I plan to read your other tale soon, it just comes out really wonky on my phone.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huge thanks to the ones looked into the tale for me.

+ And here's spoilers
- Spoilers

The Lord is the Black Lord from SCP-2264, who also happens to be SCP-035 (this is not my headcanon tho :P). He was mentioned to be banished from Alagadda in 2264, and this tale is about how he left Alagadda and ended up in Foundation's reality.

Hanged King in my headcanon, is bascially powerless on his throne, as hinted in my previous tale And so the Crows Laughed. He dropped the cup of blood he should be having, and thus not able to fully control his kingdom, and the Ambassador is actually in power. But the Black Lord, seeking more power, trying to secretly help the King get his kingdom back, but got discovered.

The Ambassador tried to kill him, but he, in this tale, escaped by possessing his servant. They later discovered that he's not actually dead, but by that time he had gotten to Foundation's reality already.

The Black Lord got rid of his body, so he exists in the form of a mask. And as the host is not his original body, he is constantly corrupting it. Mostly, this tale trying to give a back story to Black Lord, and link his banishment to his current state.

A lot of people have suggested me that the tale is a bit short of plot, but I feel like adding anything else to it will make it a completely different thing… But thanks again to those who read it, and hope it works out :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels like Mr. Brainwash binge read Chambers and Poe. Downvoted, I'm afraid.

Which is to say, it strikes me as an attempt to imitate the style of Poe/Chambers, but it lacks any of the substance or context. What is happening here? Who are these people, why do we care? There's no way for me to identify with any character, so I turn to the setting and prose, both of which are… Well, they're not bad, insomuch as grits don't taste bad.

Edit: Which is a shame because 2264 did the Chambers/Poe so well.

Double Edit: Context! I maintain that the prose needs work, but with the knowledge that the spoiler reference will be made more explicit, I now know what's going on enough to remove the downvote.
